identificationModule.briefTitle: Effect of a Virtually Implemented Cardiac Rehabilitation Program
identificationModule.officialTitle: A Clinical Trial Investigating the Effects of a Virtually Implemented Home Based Cardiac Rehab Program with Real-time, Video-based Exercise Supervision and Vitals Monitoring

descriptionModule.briefSummary: A clinical trial investigating the effects of a virtually implemented home-based cardiac rehab program with real-time, video based exercise supervision and vitals monitoring.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription: This study will use a randomized, controlled trial design to test the effectiveness of a virtually implemented cardiac rehabilitation (CR) program with real-time, video based supervised exercise and vitals monitoring as compared to traditional center based rehab in patients with a clinical indication for cardiac rehab. The primary outcome of effectiveness will be change in cardiorespiratory fitness as assessed by maximal VO2 achieved at the start of CR and on upon completion of CR.

designInfo.interventionModelDescription: Randomized, non-inferiority, Case vs. Control (1 to 1)

armGroups.label: Intervention
armGroups.description: Virtual cardiac rehab program delivered through the CardaHealth platform.
interventionNames: Other: Virtually administered Cardiac Rehab program

armGroups.label: Control
armGroups.description: Clinically ordered standard of care cardiac rehab program (in-person).
interventionNames: Other: Standard of Care in person Cardiac Rehab Program

interventions.name: Virtually administered Cardiac Rehab program
interventions.description: Virtual cardiac rehab program delivered through the CardaHealth platform.

interventions.name: Standard of Care in person Cardiac Rehab Program
interventions.description: Clinically ordered standard of care cardiac rehab program (in-person).

primaryOutcomes.description: Cardiorespiratory fitness as assessed by maximal VO2
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At baseline visit and at the end of study approximately 40 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.description: Subject's adherence to scheduled cardiac rehab sessions.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Mean number of cardiac rehab sessions completed per patient, over the course of 12 weeks or 36 sessions.
Prior to beginning of cardiac rehabilitation and then at completion of cardiac rehabilitation program approximately 36 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.description: Change in systolic and diastolic blood pressure over the duration of the study. BP will be self-assessed by the subjects at the start of each session.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Start of each cardiac rehab session, over the course of 12 weeks or 36 sessions.

secondaryOutcomes.description: Major cardiovascular events include cardiovascular hospitalizations and mortality
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Events will be collected up to 27 months post study enrollment
At completion of cardiac rehabilitation program approximately 36 weeks

secondaryOutcomes.description: Ascertained through the electronic health through routinely collected values
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Labs will be collected up to 27 months post study enrollment

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Patients who have been prescribed cardiac rehabilitation as part of their standard of care

Exclusion Criteria:

* Patients with significant exercise limitations other than cardiovascular disease
* Patients who are unable to exercise in home
* Patients with active cancer treatment
* Patients who do not have an email address or a cell phone

ancestors.term: Health Care Quality, Access, and Evaluation
